front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Priestess
front.text:
<reaction>: After you resolve at least 1 <blessing> token during a successful skill test: Draw 1 card.
<elder_sign> effect: +1. You may search the chaos bag for a <curse> token and return it to the token pool.
front.flavor_text: "I must perform my sacred duty... No matter the cost."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Living Sainthood
front.text:
Yuki Yagami deck only.
<reaction> When your turn begins: Add 1 <blessing> token to the chaos bag.
<reaction>  After your turn ends: Search the chaos bag for 1 <curse> token and return it to the token pool.
front.flavor_text: The sacred bloodline of the goddess Amaterasu was carefully preserved until a suitable vessel for her essence could be found.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: That August Night
front.text:
<rev> Put <name> into play and seal all <blessing> tokens in the chaos bag on it.
<for> When a <blessing> token would be added to the chaos bag: Seal it on this and add a <curse> token to the chaos bag, instead.
<action>: Test <willpower> (4). If you succeed, release all tokens sealed here and discard <name>.
front.flavor_text: <size 7.5>Many who undergo the shrine maiden "training" are unable to speak of it.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Courtesan
front.text:
Reduce the difficulty of skill tests you perform during parley actions by 1.
<reaction> After a non-<t>Elite</t> enemy enters play: Disengage from that enemy, if able. That enemy cannot engage you until the end of the round. (Limit once per round.)
<elder_sign> effect: +X. X is the number of enemies in play.
front.flavor_text: "The truth will set you free."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Myriad Truths
front.text:
Makoto Miyazaki deck only.
Limit 1 <t>Headgear</t> per investigator.
You get +1 <intellect> for each enemy at your location.
<reaction> When the investigation phase begins, if there is at least one enemy at your location: Gain 1 resource.
front.flavor_text: All secrets are laid bare beneath the mask's withering gaze.

front.text:
<rev> Put <name> into play in your threat area.
Your location gets +1 shroud.
<action> Spend 4 resources: Discard Porcelain Mirage.
front.flavor_text:
Whatever truth there may be in beauty, 
it remains only skin deep.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Shinobi
front.text:
<free>: Deal 1 damage to an enemy at your location that is not engaged with you. (Limit once per round)
<elder_sign> effect: +2. You may automatically evade 1 non-<t>Elite</t> enemy at your location.
front.flavor_text: "Though I may strike from the shadows, do not think I lack the courage to stand in the light."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Killer Kunoichi
front.text: Hideki Honda deck only. You get +1 Discard : Evade. Automatically evade all non-Elite enemies at your location.
front.flavor_text: Only one of Hideki's pupils has ever shown real promise.

front.text:
<rev> Put <name> into play in your threat area.
<for> After you succeed at a skill test: Take 1 direct horror. 
<action><action>: Discard Survivor's Guilt. You may activate this ability only if you control an <t>Ally</t> asset.
front.flavor_text: He couldn't save anyone. He can't even save himself.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Medium
front.text:
<reaction> When a non-weakness skill card would be placed in the discard pile of an investigator at your location: Shuffle that card into its owner's deck instead. (Limit once per round.)
<elder_sign> effect: +1. You may return a card from your discard pile to your hand.
front.flavor_text: <size 7.2>"A thousand generations live on in me."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Total Recall
front.text: Akira Arisato deck only. You may commit the top-most skill card in your discard pile as through it were in your hand. If that card would be discarded when the test ends, place it on the bottom of your deck, instead. (Limit once per turn.)
front.flavor_text: "Nothing is truly lost until it is forgotten."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Sundered Veil
front.text:
<rev> Put <name> into play with 1 doom on it.
<action> If there is no doom on <name>: Discard it.
<action>: Test <willpower> (5). If you succeed, remove 1 doom from <name>.
front.flavor_text:
Many souls that have passed from our world 
would do anything to return to it.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Wanderer
front.text:
<reaction>When you play a <t>Spirit</t> card: Reduce its resource cost by 1 or gain 1 resource.
<elder_sign> effect: +1. You may shuffle a <t>Spirit</t> card from your discard pile back into your deck.
front.flavor_text: "You won't live long enough to remember my name."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Warrior's Code
front.text:
Sakura Sanada deck only.
You gain an additional action during your turn which can only be used to engage or fight an enemy.
front.flavor_text: "Anyone can die. It's living that requires courage."

front.text:
<rev> Put <name> into play in your threat area.
Enemies in your threat area get +1 fight and you deal +1 damage when you fail a skill test while fighting an enemy engaged with another investigator.
<action> Take 2 damage or discard an <t>Ally</t> asset from your hand or play area: Discard <name>.
front.flavor_text: Death before dishonor.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Twin
front.text:
<reaction> After an enemy at your location is evaded: Draw 1 card.
<free>: Move 1 charge from Camera Obscura to another asset you control with "uses (charges)." (Limit once per round.)
<elder_sign> effect: +1. If you succeed, you may place 1 charge on Camera Obscura.
front.flavor_text: "I made a promise to my sister..."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Ethereal Viewfinder
front.text: Mayu Tachibana deck only. You get +1 . Uses (4 charges.) : Evade. Add your to your skill value for this test. If you succeed by 2 or more, you may spend 1 charge to deal 1 damage to the evaded enemy.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.text:
Hunter 2.
<pre> Bearer only.
<name> cannot be damaged unless it is exhausted.
front.flavor_text: Trapped within the perpetual twilight of Onigawa, Mayu's twin sister had aeons to realize the power of their sacred bloodline...and reclaim the fallen empire as her own.

front.text:
After you commit <name> to a skill test, you may add a <blessing> token to the chaos bag.
The performing investigator may return all <blessing> tokens resolved during this test to the chaos bag instead of the token pool after this test ends.
front.flavor_text: "We all must pay for the violence of our ancestors."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Lost Gospel
front.text: If is ready: Investigate. If this investigation is successful and you resolved a or token, discover an additional clue from your location. As an additional cost to initiate this ability, you may exhaust this to add a token to the chaos bag.
front.flavor_text: Even the greatest story ever told has two sides.

front.text: If this test is successful, you may add Nothing Else Matters to the hand of another investigator at your location instead of discarding it after this test ends.
front.flavor_text: "We're alive...and we're together."

front.text:
Parabellum does not ready during the upkeep phase.
<reaction> After you fail a skill test while fighting during your turn, exhaust Parabellum: You gain an additional action this turn which can only be used to fight or engage an enemy. 
<action>: Ready Parabellum.
front.flavor_text: If you desire peace, you must prepare for war.

front.text:
Attach <name> to your current location. Limit 1 per location.
<reaction> When your turn begins, if there are no enemies at the attached location: Add 1 <blessing> token to the chaos bag.
<reaction> If <name> is ready when an enemy enters the attached location: Reveal a random token from the chaos bag. If you reveal a <blessing> token, exhaust <name> and deal 1 damage to that enemy.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Devout Deputy
front.text:
You get +1 <willpower>. 
<reaction> After you succeed at a skill test in which you resolved a <blessing> token, exhaust Sofia Wallace: Deal 1 damage to an enemy at your location.
front.flavor_text: Blessed are the peacekeepers.

front.text: Echo 1 (until the end of the round, each other investigator may play from its owner's discard pile once.) Choose a connecting location. Each investigator may pay 1 resource to move one location towards the chosen location.
front.flavor_text: "They'll never see us coming."

front.text: Limit 1 Posture per investigator. When an enemy would attack another investigator at your location (before resolving that attack), exhaust : Fight. This action targets the attacking enemy. Ignore the retaliate keyword for this attack.
front.flavor_text: "You'll have to go through me, first."

front.text: Echo 1 (until the end of the round, each other investigator may play from its owner's discard pile once.) Search your deck for an Ally asset, then set it aside, shuffle your deck, and place the set-aside card on top of your deck. Reduce the cost of by 1 for each doom in play.
front.flavor_text: "They always did have a penchant for the dramatic..."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Noneuclidean Conduit
front.text:
Limit 1 per deck. 
<free> Discard The Obsidian Manifold: Search your deck for an asset that takes up at least 1 hand slot and draw it. You may immediately play that asset, reducing its cost by 3. Shuffle your deck.
front.flavor_text:
The surface of the cube writhes 
as it struggles to contain infinite possibilities.

front.text: Commit only to a skill test you are performing. If this test fails, you may take 1 direct damage to attempt this test again (after resolving all effects from the failed test), committing Indomitable to it.
front.flavor_text:
"Bravery simply means 
that you are afraid of the right things."

front.text:
Fast. Play only when another investigator would be assigned damage and/or horror that would defeat them.
Cancel that damage and/or horror. You may add 1 <blessing> token to the chaos bag for each point of damage or horror canceled in this way. You are defeated.
front.flavor_text: You always have something left to lose.

front.text:
Adopt and Adapt gains <wild> for each enemy at the performing investigator's location and each connecting location.
Non-<t>Elite</t> enemies at the performing investigator's location lose retaliate and alert for the duration of this skill test.
front.flavor_text: "No one knows what it means to hunt well like the hunted."

front.text:
Fast. Play after you assign damage to your investigator.
Discover 1 clue from your location.
front.flavor_text: All death is certain.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Last Ranger of August
front.text:
You get +1 <agility>.
<action> If you are not engaged with an enemy, exhaust <name>: Deal 1 damage to an enemy at your location or a connecting location.
front.flavor_text:
"I will restore the honor of my house, 
no matter what it takes."

front.text:
Play only as your first action.
Choose an unrevealed location that is connected to a revealed location and move to it. Then, if there is at least 1 clue at that location, you may take an investigate action. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.
front.flavor_text: Fortune favors the bold.

front.text: Investigators at your location draw a total of X cards, distributed as you wish. X is the shroud value of your current location.
front.flavor_text:
"The mind of your enemy 
wraps its secrets in its symbols."

front.text: Investigate. Choose a connecting location with the most clues and add the shroud value of the chosen location to your location's shroud value for this test. If you succeed, discover an additional clue from a connecting location.
front.flavor_text: All you need is a change in perspective.

front.text: Fast. Play after an enemy is defeated at your location. Investigate. You get +X for this investigation and if you succeed, you may discover up to X additional clues from your location. X is the defeated enemy's printed horror value.
front.flavor_text: <size 7.3>"All I need is a sharp knife and some smelling salts..."

front.text:
Limit 1 <t>Posture</t> per investigator. 
<reaction> When an enemy would attack you, place 1 of your clues on your location and exhaust <name>: Cancel that attack.
front.flavor_text: "What's the worst that could happen?"

front.text: <reaction> After you resolve at least 1 <blessing> or <elder_sign> token during a successful investigation, exhaust <name>: Discover an additional clue from your location.
front.flavor_text: Some things defy rational explanation.

front.text:
Fast. Uses (2 secrets).
<reaction> After you assign any amount of horror to your investigator: Place that many secrets on <name>.
<free> Spend X secrets and exhaust <name>: You get +X skill value for this test.
front.flavor_text: "No, I don't know what I'm doing... That's why it's going to work."

front.text:
Fast. Play only when your turn begins.
Discard all non-weakness cards from your hand. Until the end of the round, you may play other event cards in your discard pile as though they were in your hand. If you do and they would be discarded, shuffle them into your deck instead.
front.flavor_text: Nothing ever ends.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: The Final Theorem
front.text: <reaction> After you successfully investigate, exhaust <name>: Discover an additional clue from any revealed location with a shroud value lower than every location you have already discovered a clue from this turn.
front.flavor_text:
"The source code of the universe describes 
a purely geometric operating system."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Savvy Smuggler
front.text:
You get +1 <combat>.
<reaction> After you defeat an enemy, exhaust "Banzai" Stiletto: Gain 1 resource.
front.flavor_text: "What do we say to the tariff collectors, boys? Not today!"

front.text: <free> If you are not engaged with an enemy, exhaust <name>: Choose a revealed connecting location and test <agility> (X), where X is the chosen location's shroud value. If you succeed, move to the chosen location.
front.flavor_text: "You'll be fine. Just don't look down."

front.text: Uses (5 ammo). If has no ammo, discard it. If you are not engaged with an enemy, spend 1 ammo: Fight. Add your to your skill value for this attack. If you succeed by 2 or more, this attack deals +1 damage.
front.flavor_text: Before their use was outlawed and the art became largely forgotten, over fifty different schools of shurikenjutsu were practiced.

front.text: Before resolving chaos tokens for this test, the performing investigator may return one revealed chaos token to the chaos bag and reveal two additional chaos tokens. If this skill test is successful, the performing investigator gains 2 resources.
front.flavor_text: "Read ’em and weep!"

front.text:
Fast. Play only when a non-<t>Elite</t> enemy enters play. Attach <name> to that enemy. Limit 1 per enemy.
The attached enemy gets –1 fight. 
<reaction> After you defeat the attached enemy: Gain 2 resources.
front.flavor_text:
It is only dishonorable to attack an unaware 
adversary if you aren't getting paid for it.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Disarmed and Dangerous
front.text:
Permanent. Purchase at deck creation.
When you purchase <name>, suffer 2 physical trauma.
You have 3 additional hand slots, which can only be used to hold <t>Tool</t> assets that take up exactly 1 hand slot.
front.flavor_text: <size 7.6>The clockwork appendage contains several hidden compartments.

front.text: <reaction> As an additional cost to perform a fight action using a <t>Melee</t> asset you control, exhaust Charcoal Grease: This attack deals +1 damage.
front.flavor_text:
"A gauntlet packed with gunpowder can be a 
powerful tool...in the right hands."

front.text:
Limit 1 <t>Posture</t> per investigator. 
<reaction> After dealing damage to a non-<t>Elite</t> enemy while fighting, exhaust <name>: Automatically evade the damaged enemy.
front.flavor_text: Sometimes, it's just about sending a message.

front.text:
Uses (3 secrets). 
<free> Spend 1 secret and exhaust <name>: Fight. This attack uses <agility> instead of  <combat>. This attack may only target an enemy that is not engaged with you and automatically succeeds if targeting an enemy with 1 remaining health.
front.flavor_text: "I'm unarmed, see? Have a closer look..."

front.text: Fast. Play only when your turn begins. Echo 1 (until the end of the round, each other investigator may play from its owner's discard pile once.) Until the end of the round, whenever you take a basic fight, basic evade, or move action, you may take that action again (unless that action was taken via this effect).
front.flavor_text: Don't believe everything you see.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Two Souls
front.text: If is ready: Fight. Add your to your skill value for this attack. If you succeed by 2 or more, this attack deals +1 damage and you may exhaust to fight again. After you are attacked by an enemy during the enemy phase, exhaust : Fight. Add your to your skill value for this attack. This attack targets the attacking enemy.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Keeping Score
front.text:
<For> At the end of your turn: Test <intellect> (X), where X is the number of tokens sealed on <name>. If you fail, release all tokens sealed here. 
<reaction> When a non-<fail> chaos token would be returned to the chaos bag, if it was not released from a card, exhaust <name>: Seal that token here, instead.
front.flavor_text: "It's not gambling! It's just...counting."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Mother of Melodies
front.text:
<reaction> When an enemy would perform an attack of opportunity against an investigator at your location, exhaust <name>: Cancel that attack.
<reaction> After an enemy is evaded at your location, exhaust <name>: Gain 1 resource.
front.flavor_text: <size 7>Akane's appreciation of live music began as a child, when she observed a beautiful young bard banish a vengeful spirit from her village.

front.flavor_text: "You don't belong in this world!"

As an additional cost to play <name>, discard 2 non-weakness cards from your hand or play area.
Draw 3 cards. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity.
front.flavor_text: "There are no limits. Everything is permitted."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Dark Passenger
front.text: If is ready: Fight. You get +2 for this attack. If this attack is successful, you may take 1 damage to have this attack deal +1 damage, and if this attack defeats an enemy, you may exhaust to heal 1 damage from it. When your turn ends: If you did not successfully fight with Parasitic Host this turn, test (3). If you fail, Parasitic Host deals 1 damage to you.
<free> Exhaust <name>: Look at either the top card of the encounter deck, the top card of an investigator's deck, or the revealed side of an unrevealed connecting location.
front.flavor_text: "I have eyes everywhere."

front.text:
Uses (3 charges). 
<reaction> When an enemy is defeated at your location, spend 1 charge from <name>: Discover 1 clue at your location.
front.flavor_text: Taking a secret to your grave just isn't enough these days.

front.text:
Limit 1 <t>Posture</t> per investigator. 
<reaction> After an enemy engages you, exhaust <name>: Choose a <mystic> asset you control with "uses (charges)" and test <willpower> (X), where X is the number of charges on the chosen asset. If you succeed, place a charge on the chosen asset.
front.flavor_text: "You've already given me everything I need."

front.flavor_text: Wara ningy? are used in a number of rituals, most often combined with something from the intended recipient of a curse, such as a piece of hair.

front.text:

Choose a non-weakness asset you control and return it to its owner's hand.
front.flavor_text: "Wait. Something's wrong..."

front.text:
Fast. Play only during your turn.
As an additional cost to play <name>, you suffer 1 mental trauma.
Until the end of the round, the base value of each of your skills is equal to the amount of horror on your investigator.
front.flavor_text: If you can't beat them, f'taghn.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Defy the Stars
front.text:
Permanent. 
<reaction> When the game begins: Reveal tokens from the chaos bag until you reveal a token that isn't an <elder_sign> or <fail>. You may seal that token here.
front.flavor_text: As above, so below.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Moonlight Cleaver
front.text: When enters play: Place 1 doom on it. : Fight. Add your to your skill value for this attack. If Eventide has doom on it, this attack deals +2 damage. Otherwise, this attack deals +1 damage. If this attack defeats an enemy with doom on it, you may move that doom to Eventide.
front.flavor_text: In brightest day, or blackest night.

Fast. Play only during your turn.
As an additional cost to play <name>, discard a non-weakness asset that takes up at least 1 ally slot from your hand or play area.
Gain X resources, where X is the discarded asset's printed resource cost.
front.flavor_text: Those who never love give no hostage to fortune.

front.flavor_text: "C'mon! Just let me hold it for a minute..."

front.text:

<action><action>: Heal 1 damage. This ability may be activated by any investigator at your location.
front.flavor_text: "Get out of my kitchen!"

Fast. Play when the investigation phase begins.
Until the end of the round, other investigators may commit cards to tests you perform as though they were at your location and cards committed by other investigators to tests you perform gain <wild>.
front.flavor_text: Caution is a luxury you simply cannot afford.

Max 1 committed per skill test.
<name> gains <wild> <wild> if the performing investigator has failed at least one skill test this round.
front.flavor_text:
There is no shame in defeat, 
so long as the spirit remains unconquered.

front.flavor_text: Time and tide wait for no one.

front.flavor_text: "Failure is an excellent teacher."

front.text:

front.flavor_text:
"The greatest warrior need never fear the second 
greatest warrior; they need only fear the novice 
who has never held a weapon before."

front.text:

front.flavor_text: Time heals all wounds. It will cause many more.

front.text:
Fast. Play only during your turn.
Choose an investigator. The next action you perform this turn may be performed as if you were at the chosen investigator's location. Remove <name> from the game.
front.flavor_text: <size 7.6>"Our bonds grew together like roots underground until at last we found that we were one tree and not two."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Before I Forget
front.text:
Permanent. Exceptional. 
<reaction> Before you draw your opening hand: Search your deck for up to 5 non-weakness cards and discard them. Shuffle your deck.
front.flavor_text:
The best defense the mind has 
is simply forgetting to remember.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: Nomadic Impulse
front.text:
Permanent. 
<reaction> When your turn ends, if your current location is not the same one where you started your turn: Draw 1 card.
front.flavor_text: Not all who wander are lost.

front.text:
<reaction> After an enemy becomes engaged with you, exhaust <name>: Draw 1 card. 
<free> Take 1 direct damage and discard <name>: Draw 2 cards.
front.flavor_text:
"Blood moves the wheels of history; 
don't be afraid to spill some."

front.flavor_text: Utilized as a specialized "calculator" to forecast future events, the exact mechanics of this esoteric device are understood only by a select few.

Those who have attained true mastery of the sword 
will never find themselves without a blade.

You get +1 <willpower>. 
<reaction> After <name> enters play: Add up to 2 <blessing> tokens to the chaos bag.
front.flavor_text: "Its purpose is to remind us that we are always dangerously close to something better left unknown."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.text:

<for> After you fail a skill test: If <name> is ready, add 1 <curse> token to the chaos bag or exhaust The Believer's Burden.

front.flavor_text:
"It was much smaller, at first, but then I noticed that every time 
I began to doubt, the lattice of runes grew more labyrinthine..."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.text:
As an additional cost to play this <name>, take 1 direct damage. 
<for> At the start of your turn: Pay 1 resource or exhaust <name>.
While <name> is ready, you may ignore the first attack of opportunity you would take each turn.
front.flavor_text: "It made me an offer I couldn't refuse."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.subtitle: A Permanent Reminder
front.text:
As an additional cost to play <name>, take 1 direct damage. 
<for> After you evade an enemy: If <name> is ready, exhaust The Dragon's Dogma.
While The Dragon's Dogma is ready, you get +1 <combat>.
front.flavor_text: This painful rendering makes it impossible to forget your vows.

front.title: <unique><name>

<for> After you succeed at a skill test: Add 1 <blessing> token and 1 <curse> token to the chaos bag.
While there are at least 4 <blessing> or 4 <curse> tokens in the chaos bag, you get +1 <intellect> while investigating.
front.flavor_text: "We all cast our own shadows."

front.title: <unique><name>
front.text:
As an additional cost to play <name>, take 1 direct damage.
<for> After an enemy at your location takes damage: Exhaust <name>.
While <name> is ready and an enemy is at your location or a connecting location, you get +1 <intellect> while investigating.
front.flavor_text: Whether you remain the ash or become the phoenix is up to you.

front.title: <unique><name>
front.text:
As an additional cost to play <name>, take 1 direct damage. 
<for> If <name> is ready when you defeat an enemy: Take 1 damage or exhaust <name>.
While <name> is ready, you must use <agility> instead of <combat> while fighting.
front.flavor_text: "There are no people like me. Not anymore."

Fast. Play only during your turn.
Look at the top 1<per> cards of the encounter deck. If there are any enemy cards among the looked at cards, you gain an additional action this turn.
front.flavor_text: It's not paranoia if they really are out to get you.

front.text: After this skill test ends, add 2 <blessing> tokens to the chaos bag. If this skill test fails, also add 2 <curse> tokens to the chaos bag after this test ends.
front.flavor_text:

front.flavor_text: Though the slight chill nipping at your extremities urges you to tread carefully, you are certain you are alone here; the only sound that pierces the veil of eerie silence blanketing these woods is your own heartbeat.

back.name: Time Flies
back.text: Shuffle the set-aside Fearsome Fog encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.
back.flavor_text: This leisurely hike through the woods has proven considerably more taxing than had you expected. As you wipe some errant spittle from the corner of your mouth, you suddenly notice that your breath is visible, hanging in the air before you like an apparition for the briefest of moments before evaporating into nothingness. You're not just imagining things: the chill has grown considerably more bitter since you entered the forest. Your head begins to ache as you try to catch a glimpse of the sky through the canopy overhead. It was a beautiful morning with clear skies when you departed the village; just how long have you been in here....?

front.flavor_text: Frankly, the foggy forest frequently feels far from friendly. From underfoot, the fetid fumes of fallow, festering fecundity fuel a frantic fight-or-flight frenzy, and further forth from your flank, the fluttering of foul flies frequenting the fragile, frozen-over flower fields fully forms a flickering flash of fickle fear. Forward feels foolhardy, but furtively fleeing for freedom feels futile...

back.name: Feeling Funny
back.text:
Something strange is going on here; it's impossible to tell through the smothering fog that has rolled in, but it definitely feels darker now than when you entered. Has the sun gone down already?
The dull ache in your temple has grown to a rhythmic pounding and you're finding that it has become difficult to think, much less speak, although the growing discomfort in the back of your throat has also become concerning. It feels like your tongue has swollen to twice its normal size...
Each investigator must discard all non-weakness cards from their hand or take 2 horror.
Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

front.text: Investigators may not commit cards to tests performed by other investigators.
front.flavor_text: ...?

back.name: Not a Whisper
back.text:
Gasping for air, you fall to your knees. You can feel it now---the phantom noose tightening around your neck---but your frantic attempts to claw at it prove fruitless. As the world goes black, the last thing you see is a crimson butterfly dancing around your temple, and its gentle glow illuminating the slithering form of a tall woman emerging from a nearby fog bank...
Each surviving investigator is defeated and suffers 1 physical trauma.

front.text: Find Takeshi's burial site. When each surviving investigator has paid their respects to Takeshi, advance.
front.flavor_text: The villagers mentioned Takeshi was buried somewhere in this forest, but neglected to elaborate further. Somehow, you feel like you'll know his final resting place when you see it...

back.name: Rest in Peace
back.text: You spend a moment in silent contemplation as you examine the pauper's grave they left your friend to rot in. You suppose you ought to be grateful they bothered to drag him in here at all, but the distance between his grave markers and the others just doesn't sit right with you; it feels like an intentional slight. Perhaps there is more to Takeshi's death than you have been led to believe. Just as you resolve to return to the village and demand some answers, a mysterious light emerges from behind the cairn... Put the set-aside The Crimson Butterfly (Lighting the Way) enemy into play at The Lonely Cairn. Remove the Forest Threshold location from the game and discard any cards attached to it. Move any enemies or investigators at that location to Grotto of the Guardians. Put the two set aside Secret Shrine locations into play, unrevealed.

front.text: Chase the butterfly. When the investigators have both the blessings of the sun and moon, advance.
front.flavor_text: You find yourself entranced by the mysterious creature that has emerged from Takeshi's grave. Its softly fluttering wings emit a warm glow as it hypnotically dances around you, but when you reach out for it, it recoils from your touch. Is it afraid of you? Or leading you somewhere...?

back.name: Night and Day
back.text: Thanks to the creature's guidance, you were able to uncover a pair of shrines hidden deep within the forest. There's a strange and ominous symmetry between them, but you are unable to grok what, if any, significance they may have. As you move to depart the second shrine, you notice a wisp of light descending through the canopy above and alighting upon the old torii gate you passed earlier. Suddenly, the mote of light vanishes, and the ground below you begins to shudder violently, throwing you into the dirt. Once the tremor passes and you manage to pick yourself up off the ground, an unholy shriek echoes from somewhere within the forest. The Crimson Butterfly begins to sway back and forth in the air, nervously... Flip the Torii Gate (Forlorn, Forgotten) location to its reverse side. Put the set-aside Vengeful Echo enemy into play.

front.text:
<obj> If The Crimson Butterfly
is at the Torii Gate location, advance.
front.flavor_text: Something has come alive in the forest, and The Crimson Butterfly is clearly unsettled by it. Unfortunately, the forest feels like it has "rearranged" itself while you've been inside it, somehow obscuring the exit. You strongly feel you should find your way back to the gate...before whatever is out there finds you.

back.name: The Hidden Village
back.text: The Crimson Butterfly vanishes from sight as it passes through the gateway and a yawning portal opens between its archways, casting the forest in a pale red light. On the other side of the crimson aperture, a rustic village beckons... (1)

front.text: If it is Act 3a: Resign. You head back to the village.

front.flavor_text: The moon's pale light casts long shadows down the village's narrow alleyways and there is a rustic, time-lost quality to its decor and architecture that unnerves you; at first glance, the village seems deserted, but if the girls have been taken here, you'd better be prepared for anything...

back.name: Out of Time
back.text:
Something is very, very wrong here. You can't risk any more close calls, so you decide to retreat to the relative safety of the torii gate and regroup, but before you can even begin to flee, you feel an icy pressure tightening around your neck. You claw at the phantom grasp choking the life from you to no avail, and you feel your feet leaving the ground just before the world goes black...
Each surviving investigator is defeated and suffers 1 mental trauma.

front.text: Find a path to the manor. Investigators may spend the requisite number of clues, as group, to advance.
front.flavor_text: The manor on the hill seemed to be the source of the screams you heard, but you'll need to figure out how to carve a path through the cramped village in order to reach it... Hopefully, before it's too late.

back.name: Uninvited Guests
back.text:
You've managed to piece together a map of the village from scraps you've recovered, and it indicates the manor sits at the apex of a grand staircase at the rear of the village. You also found a note---discarded by a guard, perhaps---that explains the steps are imbued with some sort of magical enchantment for extra "security." As a result, there's a particular "trick" to navigating them, in order to deter the uninitiated...
Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.
If Endless Ascent is not in play, search the explore deck for it and put it into play, unrevealed. Shuffle the explore deck.
Flip Darkened Manor to its revealed side.

front.text: Enter the manor. If there are no clues on Darkened Manor, immediately advance.
front.flavor_text: Using the information on the notes you found, you should now be able to make it to the house on the hill if you can get to those stairs...

back.name: Welcome Home
back.text:
As you place your hand on the door to enter the manor, another shriek rings out from inside, causing the hairs on your neck to stand on end. There's no time to waste and no turning back now; cautiously, you open the door and step inside...
Add all locations with Victory X and no clues to the victory display and remove all other locations in play from the game. Investigators are not defeated by this effect. Discard any enemy or treachery cards at those locations.
Put the set-aside Silent Foyer, Moonlit Courtyard, Tatami Room, and Defiled Dojo locations into play unrevealed. Move each investigator to Silent Foyer.
Shuffle the encounter discard pile and the 3 set aside Ivory Handmaiden enemies into the encounter deck.

front.text: Search the manor for signs of the missing girls. (You will be instructed when to advance.)
front.flavor_text: The dim light scatters inside the musty mansion's narrow halls, leaving you feeling more than slightly claustrophobic. The source of the screams you heard earlier must lay further within...but is anybody actually home?

back.name: Crashing the Party
back.text: You stealthily slide the blood-soaked screen open and immediately let out an involuntary gasp. In the center of the training room at the rear of the building, the translucent spectre of a woman looms over a young girl. She struggles against the ghostly presence to no avail; phantom limbs keep her pinned to the the floor as long, flowing locks of jet black hair wrap themselves in a stranglehold around the girl's neck. Suddenly, the spirit's head cranes in your direction and you can feel her bloodshot eyes leering at you from behind the curtain of her hair... Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. Put the set-aside Mayu Tachibana (A Missing Twin?) story asset and The Pale Bride (Dearly Beloved) enemy into play at the Defiled Dojo. Check the Campaign Log. If the investigators have been marked by The Pale Bride, place 1 damage on Mayu Tachibana. Otherwise, place 1 damage on The Pale Bride.

front.text: <obj> If The Pale Bride is in the victory display, advance.
front.flavor_text: The young girl that the ghostly maiden has in her clutches must be one of the missing twins from Minakami. If you act quickly, you might still be able to save her...

back.name: In Sickness or in Health
back.text:
After a desperate scramble, you manage to break the spirit's grasp on the girl and the banshee emits one last ear-piercing shriek before dissolving into misty aether. The distant echo of wedding bells disorients you for a moment before your attention returns to the body of the young girl still writhing in pain upon the floor... 
(<res>2</res>)

back.name: Without A Paddle
back.text:
The search for Miyu has turned into a fight for your very survival. You're no nearer to discovering her location than when you started, and the encroaching fog billowing out from the gorge mocks you as it consumes the banks, concealing any possible port from your sight. The sharp pain in your temples intensifies as the ringing of bells grows louder and nearer. The world goes dark...
All surviving investigators are defeated and suffer 1 mental trauma.

front.flavor_text: The documents you discovered at the harbor indicate Mayu's sister may have been transported via the river. Miyu's out there, somewhere, and you've got a seaworthy vessel... Time to get to work.

back.name: Still Searching
back.text:
The settlements along the river seem to be responsible for generating "tributes" for someone or something called "Onigawa." Could this be why the girls were taken? Whatever this Onigawa is, you need to find it, fast. You're running out of places to look, but, as the fog parts, more areas of interest along the delta are revealed...
Put the set-aside Mistveil Strait location into play, unrevealed, to the left of the top leftmost location. For the remainder of the scenario, that location is considered to be adjacent to Mistveil Strait (and vice versa).
Put the set-aside Cascade Terminus location into play, unrevealed, to the right of the top rightmost location. For the remainder of the scenario, that location is considered to be adjacent to Cascade Terminus (and vice versa).

front.flavor_text: There's an electric tension in the air; something ancient and forgotten lingers here, and with every step your foot falls a little heavier...

back.name: Into the Eye
back.text: As you pass through the gate, a chill races down your spine. A whirlwind of pink petals sweeps across your field of view, temporarily blinding you, and after they pass you find yourself standing in a long courtyard. Beneath your feet, dirty cobblestones form a paved path to its apex, where the massive moon sits perfectly framed within a massive archway. All around you, a sprawling city unfolds, marked with scars telling of a great battle. A war horn sounds and the hollow echo of clashing steel begins to swell all around you... Put the set-aside Paraselene Plaza, Royal Palace, Desecrated Temple, Standing Stone, Overgrown Sepulcher, Confounding Switchback, Swallowtail Inn, and Spymaster's Perch locations into play, unrevealed. Shuffle the encounter discard pile and the set-aside Siege of Onigawa encounter set into the encounter deck.

front.text: At the start of the investigation phase: Shuffle all Doctrine cards in play back into the rally deck, then put the top card of the rally deck into play and resolve its Revelation effect. (Do not discard this card once it has been resolved; it remains in play until the start of the next investigation phase.)
front.flavor_text: Flickering pockets of the ruined city come to life all around you, allowing you to catch glimpses of ghostly defenders being overrun during a great battle that was apparently fought here...

back.name: Total War
back.text:
War is hell. You flinch as a ghostly siege engine crashes through a wall behind you, before immediately turning to dust. You're no military expert, but Onigawa's defenders appear to be losing this battle. Badly. How many times has this phantom conflict played out here? Has the result ever changed? Another war horn sounds in the distance; the defenders of Onigawa are rallying for one last push...
The lead investigator must search the encounter deck for each <t>Elite</t> enemy and draw them.
Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

front.text: Elite enemies in play gain Hunter 1. At the start of the investigation phase: Shuffle all Doctrine cards in play back into the rally deck, then put the top card of the rally deck into play and resolve its Revelation effect. (Do not discard this card once it has been resolved; it remains in play until the start of the next investigation phase.)
front.flavor_text: The sounds of fighting die down as Onigawa's ghostly defenders make one last desperate attempt to repel the invaders...

back.name: Routed
back.text:
All around you, skirmishes break out between the foot soldiers of the invading army and the ghostly defenders of Onigawa. A sense of inevitability grips you; this war has raged for longer than you have been alive, and you can't help but lose your nerve as the cacophonous singing of steel draws ever nearer...
Each surviving investigator is defeated.

front.text: Find a way to enter the mysterious city. (Do not advance this act until you are instructed.)
front.flavor_text: The strange fortified city overlooking the gorge is unmistakably conspicuous. The path up the sheer cliff leads to some sort of gatehouse; if Miyu was taken this way, then there must be some way inside...

back.name: Manual Override
back.text: With no small amount of effort, you manage to sabotage the rotten mechanism keeping the gates barred. You take a deep breath and brace yourself, but just before you push the gates open, a hollow sound somewhere in the distance, like the splashing of water, catches your attention. You strain to listen for it again, but silence is the only reply. It's probably nothing... Each investigator loses all of their clues. Place the set-aside Ghostly Grudges encounter set into the encounter discard pile. Check the Campaign Log. If the investigators unleashed the Maiden of the Black Water, search the encounter discard pile for the Kayako (Maiden of the Black Water) enemy and put it into play. If it is Agenda 1a, advance the current agenda.

front.text: <obj> At the end of the round, investigators may spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.
front.flavor_text: Navigating through this chaos won't be easy; many parts of the city are completely collapsed or otherwise inaccessible. The caustic sounds of war echo all around you, and Miyu could be anywhere...

back.name: Unto the Breach
back.text: Once again, you've canvassed damn near the entire city but failed to pick up Miyu's trail. It appears that members of the court took refuge inside the palace during the siege, but a magical ward sealing the entryway prevents you from accessing the interior. Just as you begin to despair, a familiar red glow begins to alight the courtyard... Put the set-aside Nightingale Antechamber into play, unrevealed. Put the set-aside The Crimson Butterfly (Blinding Beacon) story asset into play at the Royal Palace. If Yuki Yagami is one of the investigators in the campaign, that player must secretly read Scenario Interlude: Sealed Away in the Campaign Guide. If it is Agenda 2a, immediately the advance the agenda.

front.text: Find a way to enter Nightingale Antechamber. (Do not advance this act until you are instructed.)
front.flavor_text: The Crimson Butterfly is emitting some kind of energy field that appears to be "stabilizing" the time loop the city is caught in; when the apparitions approach it, they stutter and recoil, as if paralyzed. Perhaps it can help you disable the wards sealing away the palace...

back.name: The Other Side
back.text:
With The Crimson Butterfly's assistance, you are able to sabotage the wards sealing the palace entrance. Apparently exhausted by the effort, the creature falls back to your position and, together, you rush inside and bar the gates behind you, before the ghostly legions it was holding at bay can catch up with you...
(<res>1)

Apart from the ghostly girl you encountered in the entryway, there are few signs of life inside the palace, but blackened hand prints stick to the walls like ash... Did anyone actually manage to ride out the siege from within these walls?

front.flavor_text: A hideous, barrelling monstrosity has begun to stalk you through the halls of the palace, and suddenly the mysterious handprints covering every surface start to make a little more sense...

back.name: Lending a Hand
back.text: Despite your best efforts, you seem only able to temporarily delay the creature; each time you manage to hack away at one of its jaundiced limbs, another eagerly emerges from somewhere inside the writhing mass to take its place. Even worse, the acrid stench of smoke and burning wood has begun to linger in every room of the palace... Shuffle the set-aside Handy Horrors encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. Place 1 doom on each enemy in play.

front.text: The Offal Extremities gains Hunter 2. (Multiple instances of the hunter keyword do not stack; use the highest value for X.) When the enemy phase begins: Place 1 doom on The Offal Extremities.
front.flavor_text: You can feel the creature is gaining confidence in its movements; where once it awkwardly fumbled for you in the dark, it now hunts you with a sense of purpose, almost like it has a taste for you...

back.name: In Their Grasp
back.text:
You realize you're no longer even making a token attempt to search for Miyu; your attention is now completely devoted to evading the monsters patrolling the hallways of the palace. You break into a full dash and scramble to find a way out of the palace...
Each investigator who has not resigned is defeated.

front.text: Search for signs of Miyu. Investigators may spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.
front.flavor_text: A curious patchwork of red vines has apparently infested the foundations of the palace and now threatens to impede your investigation. You're not certain if that really was Miyu---you're not even certain if the girl that startled you in the entryway was real---but you've got to take your chances and explore the palace...

back.name: Dead Ends
back.text:
You keep thinking you catch glimpses of the girl you saw, watching you from the shadows, but whenever you turn to look, she's nowhere to be found. Miyu, if that really was her, seems to have hidden herself quite well...
You look down and sigh when you realize you're standing in a pool of fresh blood. As you lift your foot from the mess, you notice the shape of the puddle has a hard edge, as if it's draining through a gap in the floor...
Put the set-aside Concealed Passageway location into play unrevealed.

front.text: Explore the passageway under the palace. (Do not advance this act until you are instructed.)
front.flavor_text: You begin to take notice of which floorboards creak and moan as you step on them, and when you stoop down to peer between the slats you can make out something moving below... There's definitely a sublevel underneath the palace; you just need to find a way to access it.

back.name: Heartless
back.text:
You hold your breath as you finally work up the courage to pull the sheet back... Fortunately, lying before you is not Miyu's body, as you had feared; it is no one's body at all, actually, only a lifeless mannequin. Whoever designed this automaton went to great lengths to make it resemble a human being, but the illusion is shattered by a deep wound carved into its chest. Where its "heart" would be, there is merely a hollow, bloody chasm. You're about to release your death grip on the sheet and let it fall back over the doll's cold body in frustration, when suddenly a light begins to shine from the vacant wound. A crimson butterfly emerges from somewhere inside the artificial maiden and begins to float up towards the ceiling. You watch its red glow disappear through a gap in the wood work...but the dull pounding on the floor boards overhead snaps you out of your reverie. You swallow a lump in your throat as you come to terms with the situation; Miyu's not here. Maybe she never was. And now you have bigger problems...
Move The Offal Extremities to Undercroft Theatre.

front.text: Escape the palace! If all surviving investigators are at Orphan's Gaze, you may advance.
front.flavor_text: You hear the cracking of a wooden support beam collapsing in the entryway moments before a thick plume of black, acrid smoke stings your eyes. The palace is collapsing, probably due to the rapidly spreading fire, and now you can't get out the way you came in. You suspect---or maybe just hope---that Miyu was taken deeper into the palace, but every avenue you've explored only seems to lead to a dead end. There must be some other way out of here...

back.name: The Eyes of a Child
back.text: If the investigators have both retrieved the hanging mannequin and recovered the discarded mannequin: Acting on instinct, you place the mannequins into the sculpture's eye sockets. As you step back, a clockwork mechanism behind the wall emits a muffled clicking noise...and the child's face slides open, revealing a hidden passageway. You hurry through the opening, barely noticing the sculpture sliding shut behind you... (1) Otherwise Despite your best efforts, you cannot discern the purpose of the odd sculpture behind the altar; a hairline fracture, barely noticeable, across the meridian of the face taunts you. You notice both its eye sockets are hollow, as if something was meant to be placed there... Flip this act back over. (You may advance it again later.)

front.flavor_text: The air around you grows cold in the shadow of Mount Kokoro. The ground before you is littered with ivory flecks, and the moisture in your foggy breath sticks to your face. Lacking proper equipment, you suspect you won't last long out here...

back.name: Slither
back.text: As you explore the pass, the path ahead of you winds around a gaping ravine, and when you pass over it, you stop for a moment to glance up through a gap in the fog towards what you imagine must be the summit. Curiously, the snow cover seems to "bulge" around the mountain's peak, and wind around it its circumference in spiral. You wonder what could explain this "natural" oddity, when the moonlight pierces through the thinning fog and reflects off the strange substance, illuminating the faint presence of a hexagonal pattern etched into the ivory surface; it's reminiscent of interlocking plates, you think. Or, perhaps, scales... Attach the set-aside Orochimaru (Eternal Slumber) enemy to the Hunting Grounds section of the scenario card. It is always considered to be in play for the remainder of the scenario. Shuffle the remainder of the Slumbering Serpents encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

front.text: (Reminder: tokens have the modifier "–1, reveal another token." If two tokens are resolved during a single skill test, the test automatically fails and you do not reveal any more tokens for that test.)
front.flavor_text: Apparently, an impossibly large serpent is  using Mount Kokoro as its nesting grounds. The good news is that it seems to be sleeping, for now; there's no way you can hope to defeat it, but if you're careful, and lucky, you might be able to sneak past it...

back.name: Snakes on a Mountain
back.text: The serpent's attacks grow more relentless the nearer you get to the mountain's summit. There must be something there it doesn't want you to see, but you're not sure how long you can keep evading it, and the temperature continues to plummet as your elevation increases... If Orochimaru (Eternal Slumber) is in play, flip it. Orochimaru (Bane of August) makes an attack of opportunity against each investigator. Flip this agenda back over and place 1 doom on it for each token in the chaos bag. (It may advance multiple times.)

front.text: Explore the mountain pass. (Do not advance the act until you are instructed.)
front.flavor_text: Ahead of you, a winding path cuts through the snow bank. Numerous foot prints upon the ice suggest recent activity; if Miyu was taken this way... Well, you'd prefer not to think about it, really...

back.name: Hunting Grounds
back.text: Your march through the snow grinds to a halt and you decide to stop for a moment to catch your breath. Standing in the middle of a vast open plain, you brace yourself as the blizzard intensifies around you, building to a swelling gale of frost. Your visibility drops to near zero, and you feel suddenly aware of how vulnerable you are out here, alone and unguarded. You blink to clear the precipitation from your eyes, and when they open again, you discover you are standing in the center of a large burgundy circle that looks to have been freshly painted upon the loose snow. The twinkling of distant chimes echoes all around you... Each investigator loses all of their clues. Add 1 token to the chaos bag. Gather the set-aside Winds of Winter encounter set and spawn the set-aside Yuki-onna (The Lunar Queen) enemy at Kokoro Approach. Then, place 1 clue on Yuki-onna (from the token pool.) Shuffle the remainder of the Winds of Winter encounter set and the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

front.text: Explore the mountain pass. (Do not advance the act until you are instructed.)
front.flavor_text: You find yourself entertaining increasingly paranoid thoughts as you draw nearer to the mountain's summit. There's a prescence out there, hiding in the snowblind, you're certain of it. Where did it come from? What does it want? Why is it so cold...?

back.name: The Imprisoned Prince
back.text: As you climb out of the depths of the cavern and emerge once again into the light of the moon, the first thing you notice is a craggy expanse of stones littering a small cleft jutting out of the side of the rock face ahead of you. The stones are carved such that the formation resembles the fingers of a giant hand grasping up towards the heavens, and, at the center of the formation, a small shimmering dome crackles with energy. You step forward to take a closer look, and as you draw nearer to the formation, you begin to discern a shape swelling inside of the strange dome; it's some sort of small creature. An animal, perhaps. Though it remains motionless, the creature's body appears ethereal, shifting into and out of existence before your very eyes like a ghostly apparition. Cautiously, you draw ever closer to get a better look at it; you think you can make out its scrawny legs, some feathers, and a beak... Each investigator loses all of their clues. Add 1 token to the chaos bag. Read Scenario Interlude: The Witness in the Campaign Guide.

front.text: If each undefeated investigator has resigned, advance. (Hint: If the Yuki-onna is not in the victory display when this advances, she may continue to pursue you...)
front.flavor_text: The air grows thinner, and colder, as you climb higher and higher into the clouds. You're exhausted, but you're close now; you can feel it. Something ancient dwells among the crags of Mount Kokoro...you just need to find it before whatever is out here finds you.

back.name: Finding Shelter
back.text: If the Yuki-onna (The Lunar Queen) enemy was in play when the act advanced: Before you enter the citadel, you cautiously take one last look back into the howling blizzard. The witch that was stalking you is still out there somewhere... You can only hope that whatever this place is, she fears to tread it as much as you do... (1) If the Yuki-onna (The Lunar Queen) enemy was in the victory display when the act advanced: As you ascend the stairs to the fortress, your thoughts return to the witch that ambushed you earlier. Whatever this place is, she seemed desperate to keep you from reaching it... You can only hope you haven't made a mistake by coming here. (2)

front.flavor_text: The soot-laden air makes it difficult for you to breathe inside the volcano, and you loosen your collar as the pools of molten rock belch clouds of humid air onto your face. The temperature alone makes it dangerous for you to remain in this environment for very long...

back.name: Into the Fire
back.text: Beads of sweat sting your eyes as you stop to catch your breath. You're not sure how much longer you can keep this up; your supplies are dwindling and the intense heat is simply unbearable... Each investigator must take 2 total damage and/or horror. Flip this agenda back over. (It may advance multiple times.)

front.text: Survive the hostile environment. Investigators may spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.
front.flavor_text: The "primitive" machinery of the foundry seems to operate autonomously; a complex series of channels manipulates the lava flow towards large smelters and complex webs of gears and chains empty raw ore into carts, which race between smaller furnaces. But to what end? What has been forged here?

back.name: Toil and Trouble
back.text: The lion's share of the ore and magma seem to either be diverted to channels that service a large blackened anvil that rests on a granite platform near the volcano's zenith, or fueling an impassable flaming portal near the upper caldera. Maybe you can disrupt the mechanisms powering them...or even make the machinery work to your advantage? Put the set-aside The Firebreak and The Anvil of Creation locations into play, unrevealed. Then, the investigators must decide (choose one): Sabotage the foundry: Put the set-aside The Focusing Iris location into play, unrevealed, and remove the current act deck from the game. Put the set-aside act 2a and 3a into play as the new act deck. Commandeer the forge. Put the set-aside Molten Crucible location into play, unrevealed, and remove the current act deck from the game. Put the set-aside act 2c and 3c into play as the new act deck.

back.name: Out of Focus
back.text: The flaming sphere tracks you as you circumnavigate the bowels of the cavern, its blistering gaze eternally unblinking. The heat intensifies as you draw nearer to it and for a moment you feel kinship with the river of tormented souls caught in its gravity well. It stares through you, silently judging you, and then...something inside your chest decompresses, like a rush of air escaping through a pinhole. The lens of the eye cracks, and then shatters like glass, sending its smoldering shards tumbling into the abyss below. The cavern rumbles, nearly throwing you from your precarious perch, and you barely manage to leap back to solid ground before the lava field cools and hardens into a charred glacier of bone and ash... From your vantage, you can barely catch a glimpse of the flaming portal at the apex of the volcano in time to watch its fiery barrier recede...and a dark figure emerge from the shadows beyond. A torrent of spirits seems to circle the grim weapon it carries, and you feel the ground sizzling beneath your feet... Each investigator must draw a copy of the set-aside Boiling Point treachery and put it into play in their threat area. Put the set-aside Kurama Tengu (The Molochean Hand) into play at The Firebreak. Check the Campaign Log and place X doom on Kurama Tengu, where X is the number of Champions of Onigawa laid to rest. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

front.text:
<for> When the enemy phase begins: If no investigator is at Kurama Tengu's location, advance the current agenda.
<obj> If all surviving investigators are at The Firebreak and Kurama Tengu is in the victory display, advance.
front.flavor_text: The way is clear, but now you find yourself pursued by a mysterious warrior. His blackened armor superficially resembles some of the designs you saw on Onigawa's defenders during the siege on the palace, and yet...

back.name: Challenge Accepted
back.text:
The mysterious interloper shrugs off your blows and launches his own attacks without honor or humanity, but you are able to get the better of him through cunning; you feint and bait a counterattack from him that leaves him off balance near the edge of a cliff, and deliver a follow-up strike that manages to dislodge his footing.
Like a shooting star, he plummets through the air and into a pool of lava below. A massive column of fire ejects from where he impacted...followed by a hollow warcry that echoes off the rim of the volcano as spectral motes of light ascend past you, flying towards the crimson moon. Hopefully, that's the last you'll see of him, whoever he might have been...
(<res>1)

front.text: Search for the means to reactivate the forge. (Do not advance this act until you are instructed.)
front.flavor_text: Yes, the foundry has fallen into, shall we say, "disrepair," but you can feel the history thrumming through the walls of the volcano; great arms and armor were once forged here, meant for heroes and the champions of gods. Perhaps, with a little help, they might do so again...

back.name: Smoke Break
back.text: You watch the magma sluice down the crucible's trough, and enter the furnace situated above the blackened anvil below. The molten rock then funnels into a pair of molds above the anvil, which sizzle with the intense heat... While you prepare to let your guard down while the machinery does its work, the lava surrounding the granite platform suddenly begins to boil over, and a massive creature emerges with a bestial bellow, clearly angered by your attempt to "appropriate" its tools... Each investigator must draw a copy of the set-aside Boiling Point treachery and put it into play in their threat area. Put the set-aside "Sensei" Kanuti (Master of the Forge) into play at The Anvil of Creation. Check the Campaign Log and place X doom on "Sensei" Kanuti, where X is the number of Champions of Onigawa laid to rest. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

front.text:
<for>  When the enemy phase begins: If no investigator is at "Sensei" Kanuti's location, advance the current agenda.
<obj> If all surviving investigators are at The Anvil of Creation and
"Sensei" Kanuti is in the victory display, advance.
front.flavor_text: Somehow, you get the sense that this massive creature is the master of the forge. Its ceremonial armor resembles an oversized version of some of the mantles you observed on Onigawa's defenders during the siege, and yet...

back.name: Forged in Flame
back.text:
Eventually, the giant relents, and, with a wounded groan, it slinks away, once again submerging itself beneath the surface of the lava.
As if on cue, the molds suspended above the anvil shatter, and when the molten blades come to rest upon the anvil's blackened surface you know what must be done; raising a nearby hammer over your head, you spend a long while furiously pounding the metal into shape, unlocking their potential...
(<res>2)

front.subtitle: The Last Ward
front.text: Spend X clues: Fight. You may use your in place of your for this test. This attack may target an enemy up to X locations away.
front.flavor_text:
Obsidian stairs lead to a flaming portal 
at the tip of a massive stone blade...

back.flavor_text: Near the upper rim of the caldera, you can make out the silhouette of a giant sword cleaving the earth...

front.subtitle: The Aspect Forge
front.text: <t>Aspect</t> story assets controlled by investigators at <name> do not ready during the upkeep phase.
front.flavor_text:
Legends say the souls of the Guardians 
were forged upon this very anvil.

back.flavor_text: Suspended above the molten front, a mysterious granite platform bears the weight of a blackened anvil...

front.subtitle: The Eye of Izanami
front.text: If it is act 2a and there are no clues on , exhaust an Aspect story asset you control: An eye for an eye. Advance the act.
front.flavor_text: The demigod Izanagi sacrificed his eyes to create the sun and moon when his consort, Izanami, became lost to the underworld. Few know that she responded, in kind...

back.flavor_text: It is believed that fire first was brought to this land when the guardian Tsurugi, in need of a flame to temper his blade, plunged his greatsword into the virgin soil and cleaved Mount Kokoro from the countryside...

front.text: If it is act 2c and there are no clues on , discard 3 cards from your hand: Manual override. Advance the act.
front.flavor_text: A series of mechanisms seems to allow you to redirect the lava flow; perhaps you can use it to feed the forge?

back.flavor_text:
A hovering platform near the far wall seems 
to collect runoff from one of the upper lava pools 
and divert it into a series of channels...

front.flavor_text: There's something unsettlingly familiar about one of the faces within the writhing mass of rendered fat...

Massive. Alert. Patrol (The Anvil of Creation)

front.flavor_text: Kanuti was a friendly giant who inhabited these lands when the Guardians were young. After they rose to power, Tsurugi recognized Kanuti's strength by "honoring" him with a lifetime appointment as his personal forgemaster.

front.flavor_text:
Do not wait to strike until the iron is hot; 
you must make it hot by striking.

front.text: When the mythos phase ends: Perform a Sacrifice.
front.flavor_text: The strange priest who has abducted Miyu is performing a profane ritual to drain the girl of her essence and summon his dark master to this world. To fuel the ritual and strengthen the magical barrier protecting them, wayward souls are being called to the gallows from all corners of Onigawa, across all time and space...

back.name: Minutes to Midnight
back.text: As more and more souls are sacrificed upon the cathedral's Profane Altar, the Shepherd's body begins to pulse, phasing in and out of existence as the ritual's power intensifies. A spectral silhouette, from some hellish place you cannot begin to fathom, emerges from the aether and gradually begins to take the shepherd's place... You're running out of time. Moloch is close... Shuffle the Shepherd of the Black Harvest into the sacrifice deck. Place 1 doom on Miyu Tachibana (Lost and Found). Flip the Sundered Cathedral to its revealed side. Put the set-aside Moloch (The Infernal Engine) into play at Profane Altar. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

front.text: When the mythos phase ends: Perform a Sacrifice. Do not remove doom from any other card in play when this agenda advances.
front.flavor_text: With every sacrifice that fuels his resurrection, Moloch's intangible essence grows closer to gaining a physical foothold into this world...

back.name: Speak of the Devil
back.text: Miyu gasps: the sound of a death rattle. You can feel her slipping away, and, empowered by the senseless slaughter, Moloch's flickering shadow begins to take form... Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. Flip Profane Altar to its revealed side. Flip Moloch (The Infernal Engine) to its reverse side. If Miyu Tachibana (Lost and Found) is at the Profane Altar location, move all doom on her onto Moloch (The Ghost of Onigawa), then shuffle Miyu Tachibana into the sacrifice deck.

front.text: When the mythos phase ends: Perform two Sacrifices.
front.flavor_text: Despite your amateur interventions---or, perhaps, because of them---Moloch walks the world once more, and he has no purpose other than to gorge himself on Onigawa's misery. Creation itself trembles; without the strength of the Guardians left to oppose him, is there any hope...?

back.name: In the End
back.text:
It's no use. Like the Stewards of August before you, the Lunar Queen, and even the Guardians themselves, you have only succeeded in delaying Moloch's inevitable coming. He is fear made flesh and nothing can stop him now...
Woe to the conquered.
(<res>4</res>)

front.text: If there are no clues at your location: Flip the Torii Nexus location to its revealed side. (Group limit once per game.) Investigators at Torii Nexus may spend the requisite number of clues, as a group, to advance.
front.flavor_text: The cataclysm has separated you from both your companions and from Miyu. You need to find some way to regroup...

back.name: The Stars Above
back.text:
You've managed to achieve passage to several of the other floating islands with help from the Torii Gate, but none seem to get you any closer to the cathedral. You turn around and prepare to return to the shelf and come up with a new plan, when you realize that the torii gates on each island have all been perfectly aligned with the rune-marked moon...
You pull out the Camera Obscura and attempt to photograph the moon through the torii gate nearest to you. When the shutter clicks, the image in the portal instantly changes to a lifeless, rocky expanse...
Put the set-aside Harvest Moon into play unrevealed.

front.text: <obj> If there are no clues on the Harvest Moon location, advance.
front.flavor_text: It's been there, all along, watching you, and it's helped---or hampered---your investigation every step of the way. Maybe the answers you seek are written in the stars...or maybe you've just finally lost your mind?

back.name: A Giant Leap
back.text: Well, you did it: you went to the moon. And, surprisingly, nothing abjectly terrible happened to you as a result. It's surprisingly hot up here, though, and the moon is still high enough in the sky that the thin oxygen levels are starting to give you a migraine. Not for nothing, but, why are you even here...? You manage to bounce around the moon's rocky surface until at last you reach the center of the rune carved into its surface. Inside an enormous crater, a strange object smolders like charcoal... Put the set-aside Smoldering Spawn (What Was Lost in the Fire) into play at the Harvest Moon location.

front.text: <obj> If there are 4 or more <blessing> tokens sealed on Smoldering Spawn, advance.
front.flavor_text: The bloody crescent on the moon's surface appears to be some kind of oval container nestled in the center of a large crater. It's warm to the touch... Perhaps there's some way to open it...?

back.name: Gods Among Us
back.text: You watch the egg-like object siphon away the energy you've worked so hard to generate, like some sort of karmic sinkhole. You're about to abandon hope, when suddenly, it cracks, and a pillar of flame ejects from the surface of moon... Release all tokens sealed on Smoldering Spawn and remove it from the game. Shuffle all six Avatar story assets from the Forged in Flames encounter set together, and then check the Campaign Log and proceed to the relevant section below: If the investigators sabotaged the sacred foundry: Reveal three of the gathered cards at random. The lead investigator must choose one to put into play at Harvest Moon. Remove the unchosen cards and any unrevealed cards from the game. If the investigators used the Anvil of Creation: Choose one of the gathered cards at random and put it into play at Harvest Moon. Remove the rest of the cards from the game.

front.text: Reminder -- Harmony tokens count as both a and a token and, during skill tests the performing investigator may choose which of the two to resolve them as. Resolved harmony tokens are returned to the chaos bag after the test ends. If there are 8 harmony tokens in the chaos bag, or Moloch is in the victory display, advance.
front.flavor_text: You would not believe it had you not seen it with your own two eyes. The relic upon the moon's surface was no mere anomaly; it was the sacred vessel for a Guardian's rebirth...

back.name: What Was Promised
back.text: If Moloch (The Ghost of Onigawa) was in the victory display when this advanced: Moloch howls as he reels from your relentless assault; he clearly did not expect you to be equipped to harm him. His burgundy skin turns to ash as his depraved essence begins to lose purchase on his physical form. The ground below you begins to shudder; in the confusion, you quickly grab Miyu from the altar before hurling yourself into the nearest torii gate... (2) If there were 8 harmony tokens in the chaos bag when this advanced: Empowered by your ministrations, the reborn Guardian swoops down upon the cathedral to engage Moloch head on. Crackling bolts of magical energy arc across the landscape as they trade blows, and the impact of their duel hurls you into the portal of a nearby torii gate... (1)

Pieces of the cathedral are now scattered across

Once upon a time, it brought you to this place.... 
Maybe, when it's all over, it can send you home.

back.flavor_text:
You notice each floating island contains 
its own Torii gate that perfectly aligns with the moon...

Streaming waterfalls pour off the edges

The thermal energy produced by the cataclysm 
seems to have caused the glaciers on these 
floating chunks of Mount Kokoro to thaw...

front.subtitle: The Future Refused to Change

"There was, in their city, a bronze image of Cronus...

rolled down and fell into a gaping pit filled with fire..."
---Bibliotheca Historica

Onigawa was not the first civilization that 
Moloch has brought to heel; through these 
collapsed and decaying arches, you can catch fleeting glimpses of times and places long since past...
